info.name: Shiplogic API
info.description:
# **Welcome to the Shiplogic API!**

Our REST API provides predictable, resource-oriented URLs, uses standard HTTP methods, and returns JSON-encoded responses with conventional HTTP status codes. It’s designed to be intuitive, reliable, and easy to integrate with modern systems.

Please use the API responsibly and within the boundaries of Shiplogic’s [acceptable use policy](https://shiplogic.com/acceptable-use-policy). This means accessing only resources and data that you are authorised to view, using endpoints as intended, and avoiding excessive or automated requests that could impact service stability or other users. Continued use of the API implies acceptance of these terms and responsible usage practices.

item.name: Authentication
item.description:
We use Bearer Token authentication for our API. To authenticate, simply include your token in the header of each request, like so:

"Authorization": "Bearer a601d99c75fc4c64b5a64288f97d52b4"

You can explore and test our API in a dedicated sandbox environment called **Sandbox Couriers**.

To get started, [register a free sandbox account here](https://sandbox.shiplogic.com/register).

Once you’ve created your account and verified your email address, log in and generate an API key under **Settings > API Keys**.

⚠️ When you’re ready to go live, you’ll need to register a production account with the relevant courier and create a new API key for use in the live environment.

item.name: HTTP responses
item.description:
## Response format

The Shiplogic API returns data in application/json format. To reduce payload size and improve performance, **fields with falsy values may be omitted** from the response.

## Response codes

#### **200 OK**

Returned for all successful GET, PUT, PATCH, or POST requests.

#### **204 No Content**

Returned for all successful DELETE requests.

#### **400 Bad Request**

Returned when:  
• Request validation fails (e.g. a field has the wrong type or a required field is missing).  
• An invalid action is attempted (e.g. cancelling a shipment that has already been collected).

A descriptive error message is included in the response.

#### 401 Unauthorised

Returned when the user cannot be found.  
• Response message: "Error logging in".  
• Common cause: The API user has been deleted, but their API key is still in use.

#### 403 Forbidden

The user does not have permission to perform the action. A descriptive message is included.

#### 404 Not Found

Returned when an invalid endpoint is requested (e.g. [https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments/invalid](https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments/invalid)).  
Also used in rare cases where a resource cannot be found, though this is not typically expected by clients.

#### 405 Method Not Allowed

Returned when a valid endpoint is used with an invalid HTTP method (e.g. PUT on [https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments](https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments)).

#### 422 Unprocessable Entity

Returned when the server understands the request but cannot complete the action.  
Currently, this only occurs when attempting to email CSV or PDF files to an API user (API users do not have email addresses).

#### 423 Locked

Returned when requests are made with a user belonging to a closed account.

#### 429 Too Many Requests

An API user exceeds their request rate limit.

#### 500 Internal Server Error

Returned when a server-side issue occurs (e.g. database access failure).  
This error is not expected during normal operation.

### Paging

Most GET endpoints support pagination with the limit and offset query parameters.  
Note: the count property in responses is capped at 10,000 for performance reasons.

item.name: Shipment statuses
item.description:
Here’s a short description of each of the Shiplogic shipment statuses:

**Collection statuses**

- **collection-assigned** – A driver has been allocated to collect the shipment.
    
- **collection-unassigned** – No driver is currently assigned for collection.
    
- **collection-rejected** – The assigned driver has rejected the collection (typically due to incorrect assignment).
    
- **collection-exception** – An unexpected issue occurred during collection (e.g. address problem).
    
- **collection-failed-attempt** – Collection was attempted but not successful.
    
- **collected** – The shipment has been successfully picked up.
    
- **awaiting-dropoff** – The shipment is waiting to be dropped off by a customer (used for lockers, kiosks and counters).
    

**Hub & transit statuses**

- **at-hub** – The shipment has arrived at a hub or depot.
    
- **on-hold** – The shipment is on hold pending resolution (e.g. query, instruction).
    
- **on-hold-internal** – Internal hold applied by the courier for operational reasons.
    
- **returned-to-hub** – Returned back to the hub after a failed delivery.
    
- **manifested** – The shipment has been included in an inter-hub manifest.
    
- **ready-for-dispatch** – Ready to leave the hub toward the next hub.
    
- **in-transit** – Currently in transit between hubs.
    
- **at-destination-hub** – Arrived at the hub closest to the final delivery point.
    

**Delivery statuses**

- **delivery-assigned** – A driver has been assigned for delivery.
    
- **delivery-unassigned** – Delivery driver not yet assigned.
    
- **delivery-rejected** – The assigned driver rejected the delivery (typically due to incorrect assignment).
    
- **out-for-delivery** – The shipment is with the driver on the way to the recipient.
    
- **delivery-exception** – An issue occurred during delivery (e.g. address issues).
    
- **delivery-failed-attempt** – Delivery was attempted but unsuccessful.
    
- **ready-for-pickup** – The shipment is ready to be picked up by a customer (used for lockers, kiosks and counters).
    
- **delivered** – Successfully delivered to the recipient.
    

**Special statuses**

- **returned-to-sender** – Returned to the sender after failed delivery attempts.
    
- **undeliverable** – Cannot be delivered or returned (e.g. damaged, recipient unknown).
    
- **cancelled** – Shipment cancelled before completion.
    
- **floor-check** – Shipment was physically verified in the facility (floor check).
    

The following statuses aren't shown to end-users and are mapped visually to other statuses:

- **collection-unassigned** to **collection-assigned**
    
- **collection-rejected** to **collection-assigned**
    
- **delivery-unassigned** to **delivery-assigned**
    
- **delivery-rejected** to **delivery-assigned**
    
- **on-hold-internal** to **at-hub**

item.name: Webhooks
item.description:
Subscribe to specific webhook topics to receive webhooks for certain events.

## Account webhooks

Account owners can configure webhooks for the following events by going to Settings > Webhook subscriptions.

### Shipment note

``` json
{
  "attachments": { "uploads": [] },
  "id": 0,
  "message": "The sender does not want to handover the parcel",
  "shipment_custom_tracking_reference": "SLXS7GL",
  "shipment_id": 108639,
  "shipment_short_tracking_reference": "S7GL",
  "time_created": "2025-06-17T12:20:39.452Z",
  "time_modified": "2025-06-17T12:20:39.836149Z",
  "type": "external"
}

 ```

### Tracking event

``` json
{
  "collection_hub": "JHB",
  "collection_lat": -25.785945,
  "collection_lng": 28.314838,
  "custom_tracking_reference": "SLXS7GL",

  "delivery_lng": 28.278871,
  "event_time": "2025-06-17T14:23:30.493767538+02:00",
  "parcel_tracking_references": ["SLXS7GL/1"],
  "provider_id": 1,
  "service_level_code": "ECO",
  "shipment_collected_date": "2025-06-17T12:22:32.630888Z",

    {

 ```

### Parcel dimension changes

``` json
[
  {
    "provider_id": 1,    "shipment_id": 101258638,
    "shipment_tracking_reference": "G9MR6V",
    "parcel_reference": "G9MR6V/1",
    "parcel_alternative_tracking_reference": "",
    "source": "stephanadmin",
    "event_type": "manual",

]

 ```

### Shipment address changes

{
  "new_delivery_address": {

## Admin webhooks

Third-party integrations with the "Webhook" integration type will send an entire shipment's payload when the integration is triggered. This payload is equivalent to the shipment found in the JSON response of the [https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments](https://api.shiplogic.com/shipments) GET endpoint. These webhooks are admin-level webhooks that should be used with caution, as they contain sensitive shipment details such as POD PINs.

Triggers:

- Shipment is created
    
- Shipment is updated
    
- Shipment is scanned by hub scanner
    
- Shipment becomes outbound

item.name: Addresses
item.description:
Addresses are central to Shiplogic and is used for getting rates, submitting shipments etc.

The structure of an address can be seen below:

```
{
        "company": "Shiplogic",
        "street_address": "194 Bancor Avenue",
        "type": "business",
        "local_area": "Menlyn",
        "city": "Pretoria",
        "zone": "Gauteng",
        "country": "ZA",
        "code": "0181",
        "lat": -25.7863272,
        "lng": 28.277583
}

 ```

Ideally pass through the `lat` and `lng` (latitude and longitude) for best accuracy. If the `lat` and `lng` is not passed through, Shiplogic will attempt to geocode the address to the best of its ability. The `type` field (optional) can be `residential`, `business` , `counter` or `locker`. If no `type` is provided it will default to `unknown`.

item.name: Accounts API
Shipping and rates

request.description:
Use this endpoint to retrieve the relevant rates and service levels for a given `account_id`, `collection_address`, `delivery_address` and list of `parcels`. Optionally, include a `declared_value` (for insurance purposes), possible user `opt_in_rates` e.g. Gift wrapping and `opt_in_time_based_rates` e.g. Early-bird delivery.

See the "Getting opt-in rates" on how to get the possible `opt-in rates` and `opt-in time based` rates for a given `collection_address` and `delivery_address`. `opt_in_rates` and `opt_in_time_based_rates` should be specified as an array of ID's e.g. `"opt_in_time_based_rates":[76]`.

The following fields are required:

- `collection_address`
    
- `delivery_address`
    
- `parcels`
    

See the structure of addresses at the main section at the start of the documentation.

Certain special request options are available to add to a shipment e.g. Early bird deliveries or a gift wrapping service. This is dependant on how the courier has set up their rates.

Send the `collection_address` and `delivery_address` to this endpoint to get a list of `opt_in_rates` and `opt_in_time_based_rates` which can be sent to the `POST /rates` and subsequently to the `POST /shipments` endpoint (above).

The following fields are required:
- `collection_address`
- `delivery_address`

See the structure of addresses at the main section at the start of the documentation.

body.raw:
{
    "collection_address": {
        "type": "business",
        "company": "Shiplogic",
        "street_address": "377 Fairy Glen St",
        "local_area": "Lynnwood Park",
        "city": "Pretoria",
        "zone": "Gauteng",
        "country": "ZA",
        "code": "0081"
    },
    "collection_contact": {
        "name": "Cornel Rautenbach",
        "mobile_number": "",
        "email": "cornel+sandy@shiplogic.com"
    },
    "delivery_address": {
        "type": "residential",
        "company": "",
        "street_address": "10 Midas Ave",
        "local_area": "Olympus AH",
        "city": "Pretoria",
        "code": "0081",
        "zone": "Gauteng",
        "country": "ZA",
        "lat": -25.80665579999999, // Optional, but recommended
        "lng": 28.334732 // Optional, but recommended
    },
    "delivery_contact": {
        "name": "",
        "mobile_number": "",
        "email": "james+sandyreceiver@shiplogic.com"
    },
    "parcels": [
        {
            "parcel_description": "Standard flyer",
            "submitted_length_cm": 20,
            "submitted_width_cm": 20,
            "submitted_height_cm": 10,
            "submitted_weight_kg": 2,
            "alternative_tracking_reference": "USUALLYATRACKINGSTICKER" // Optional
        }
    ],
    "opt_in_rates": [],
    "opt_in_time_based_rates": [
        76
    ],
    "special_instructions_collection": "This is a test shipment - DO NOT COLLECT",
    "special_instructions_delivery": "This is a test shipment - DO NOT DELIVER",
    "declared_value": 1100,
    "collection_min_date": "2025-05-21T00:00:00.000Z",
    "due_date": "2025-05-24T00:00:00.000Z",
    "collection_after": "08:00",
    "collection_before": "16:00",
    "delivery_min_date": "2025-05-21T00:00:00.000Z",
    "delivery_after": "10:00",
    "delivery_before": "17:00",
    "custom_tracking_reference": "",
    "customer_reference_name": "Order no.",
    "customer_reference": "ORDERNO123",
    "service_level_code": "ECO",
    "mute_notifications": false
}

request.description:
The following fields are required:

- `collection_address`
    
- `collection_contact`
    
- `delivery_address`
    
- `delivery_contact`
    
- `parcels`
    
- either `service_level_code` or `service_level_id` (returned when retrieving rates)
    

See the structure of addresses at the main section at the start of the documentation.

The `collection_contact` and `delivery_contact` requires at least an `email`and/or `mobile_number`.

`mute_notifications`: If this property is set to `true`, no SMS and/or email notifications will be sent on behalf of this shipment.

`customer_reference`: Any reference you want to add to the shipment e.g. an order number or external ID you want to associate with the shipment.

`customer_reference_name`: This optional field describes what the customer reference refers to e.g. `PO number`, `Order no.`, `Internal ref`, etc.

Optionally, include a `declared_value` (for insurance purposes), possible user `opt_in_rates` e.g. Gift wrapping and `opt_in_time_based_rates` e.g. Early-bird delivery.

See the "Getting opt-in rates" on how to get the possible `opt-in rates` and `opt-in time based` rates for a given `collection_address` and `delivery_address`. `opt_in_rates` and `opt_in_time_based_rates` should be specified as an array of ID's e.g. `"opt_in_time_based_rates":[76]`.

The `collection_min_date` and `delivery_min_date` can be used if you'd like the collection or delivery to take place only at a later stage.

`due_date` is a field used for reporting purposes. It doesn't change anything operationally.

On a parcel, set an optional `alternative_tracking_reference` if you have preprinted tracking stickers or a pre-defined barcode that will be linked to the parcel.

query.description: The short or custom tracking reference of the shipment

request.description:
This endpoint returns a list of shipments or a specific shipment if a tracking reference or shipment ID is passed through as a query parameter.

## Other query parameters:

To filter shipments with a creation date within a date range send through the following query parameters:

`?date_filter=time_created&start_date=2022-01-11 12:52:20+02:00&end_date=2022-01-25 23:59:59+02:00`

The following options are available for the `date_filter` query parameter:

*   `time_created`
*   `collected_date`
*   `delivered_date`
    

To filter on shipments statuses send through the `status` parameter with a list of statuses you would like to include e.g. `?status=["collected","delivered"]` .
This endpoint takes an `id` as a query parameter (the shipment's `id`, not the `provider_shipment_id`) and returns a signed URL to a PDF on a AWS S3 bucket. The URL expires in 24 hours.
This endpoint takes an `id` as a query parameter (the shipment's `id`, not the `provider_shipment_id`) and returns a signed URL to a PDF on a AWS S3 bucket. The URL expires in 24 hours.
This endpoint cancels the shipment. If the shipment hasn't been collected all charges are automatically reversed.

item.description: This sections lists all the API endpoints related to getting rates and generating, updating, retrieving and cancelling shipments (also known as waybills).

item.name: Get signed URL to download a file

request.description:
Use this endpoint to get signed URLs to POD images. POD file names can be found in tracking events. See the `Tracking a shipment` GET request for more information.

Please note that the `file_name` parameter is an array of filenames and `folder` has a constant value of `shipment-images`

This endpoint takes a tracking reference as a query parameter and return the shipment's status, together with an array of tracking events for that shipment.

On each event there is a property called `parcel_id`. If the `parcel_id` is 0, the event refers to the shipment status. If the `parcel_id` > 0, the status refers to the appropriate parcel of the shipment.

Please use this endpoint responsibly: Only when needed and only if you’re authorised to access the information.

## PODs

If a shipment is delivered, a POD can be provided in a few ways. The `message` property of the `tracking_event` can be used to determine the POD method used:

- `POD image captured`
    
- `Recipient details entered`
    
- `PIN entered successfully`
    

If the message is `POD image captured`, the actual POD images can be found within the `data` property with the following structure:

```
"tracking_events": [
  {
    "id": 332,
    "parcel_id": 0,
    data: {
        type: "proof-of-delivery-images",
        images: ["442-aff78c7a-b08a-45c7-b46d-67e3fae607fa.jpg"],
        pdfs: ["233-bef78c7a-ag43-45c7-b46d-67e3fae607fa.pdf"]
      },
    ...
  }
]

 ```

The POD can either be a set of images and/or PDFs. The files are stored on S3 and requires a signed S3 URL. See the `POD image downloads` section.

## Possible tracking statuses

```
"submitted",
"awaiting-dropoff",
"collection-exception",
"collection-failed-attempt",
"collected",
"at-hub",
"swad-dimensions",
"swad-imaging",
"returned-to-hub",
"in-transit",
"at-destination-hub",
"out-for-delivery",
"delivery-exception",
"delivery-failed-attempt",
"in-locker",
"ready-for-pickup",
"collect-and-return-to-hub",
"collected-from-locker",
"collected-from-counter",
"delivered",
"cancelled",
"floor-check"

 ```

item.name: Getting POD images for a shipment

request.description:
This endpoint takes either a `tracking_reference` (of the waybill) or a `shipment_id` as a query parameter and returns an array of tracking events that contains signed POD images (and other metadata).

To include the generated digital POD in the response, add the `include_digital_pod` query parameter with a value of `true` to the request.

item.name: Getting all POD events for a shipment

request.description:
This endpoint takes either a `tracking_reference` (of the waybill) or a `shipment_id` as a query parameter and returns an array of tracking events that contains all events related to delivery. This includes entering the PIN successfully, POD files captured etc. for the shipment, as well as the individual parcels of the shipment.

To include the generated digital POD in the response, add the `include_digital_pod` query parameter with a value of `true` to the request.

item.name: Getting a digital POD for a shipment

query.description: The short or custom tracking reference of the shipment

request.description:
This endpoint takes a `tracking_reference` (of the waybill) or a `shipment_id` as a query parameter and returns a URL that can be used to download a digital POD for a shipment. Digital PODs contain the delivery information of a shipment or parcel.

A digital POD is generated for the latest delivered tracking event on the shipment. You can optionally provide a `tracking_event_id` to generate a digital POD for any other tracking event, including parcel-specific tracking events. To obtain these tracking event IDs, see the `Getting all POD events for a shipment` section. Note that for a digital POD to be generated, the delivered tracking event needs to have a `lat` and `lng`.
This endpoint takes a date range and account ID as a query parameters and returns a signed URL to a statement PDF on a AWS S3 bucket. The URL expires in 24 hours.
This endpoint takes a date range and account ID and returns a list of credit notes.
This endpoint takes a credit note ID as a query parameter and return all the list items for the specified credit note.
This endpoint takes a date range and account ID and returns a list of invoices.
This endpoint takes an invoice ID as a query parameter and return all the list items for the specified invoice.
This endpoint takes an account ID (and optional sorting and paging parameters) as query paramaters and returns an array of billing transactions.

item.description: This section describes the API calls that can be made by an account user or on behalf of an account as an administrator.

item.name: Admin API

request.description: This endpoint returns all notes on an account.

item.name: Adding a note to an account

request.description: This endpoint adds a note to the specified account.
This note deletes a note from an account. Only the creator of the note can delete the note.

This endpoint returns all the public holidays set up by the courier. Public holidays are excluded from collection and delivery estimates unless a specific "Public holiday" time-based rate adjustment is set up.

This endpoint sets the public holidays for a courier. Please note that the array passed through to the endpoint will be the complete list of public holidays. Existing public holidays will be removed if not present in the array received.
This endpoint returns a list of users. You can also specify an `account_id` instead of a `username` to fetch all the users for an account.
This endpoint returns a list of addresses book addresses for the specified account ID.

item.name: Adding an address to the address book

request.description: This endpoint adds an address to the address book of the specified account ID.

item.name: Removing an address from the address book

request.description: This endpoint removes the address with the specified ID from the account's address book.
This endpoint returns a list of accounts, or if an ID is specified a single account's details.

This endpoint creates an account with a specified name, default collection address and the first Account super user. The Account super user will receive an email with instructions and can log in on https://sandbox.shiplogic.com
This endpoint returns an array of courier-wide settings.

request.description:
Create a billing transaction for an account. This can be used to post charges, credits, payments, refunds, and balance adjustments to the account statement.

## Authentication

Requires a valid JWT Bearer token in the Authorization header.

## Required fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| type | string | The billing transaction type. See allowed values below. |
| description | string | Description shown on the account statement. |
| amount | number | Transaction amount. |
| account_id | integer | The account ID to post the transaction against. |
| provider_id | integer | The courier's provider ID. |

## Optional fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| shipment_reference | string | Optional shipment reference (waybill number) linked to the transaction. |

## Allowed type values

- payment
    
- payment-reversal
    
- refund
    
- refund-reversal
    
- admin-debit
    
- admin-credit
    
- promotional-debit
    
- promotional-credit
    
- balance-adjustment-debit
    
- balance-adjustment-credit
    
- bad-debt-write-off
    
- bad-debt-write-off-reversal

item.name: Inter-hub manifests

request.description: This endpoint returns a list of shipments which is part of the specified inter-hub transfer, together with their parcels scanned.

item.name: Adding a parcel to the manifest

request.description: This endpoint adds the specified parcel tracking reference to the specified inter-hub transfer.

item.name: Removing a parcel from the manifest

request.description: This endpoint removes the specified parcels (using their IDs and not references) from the specified inter-hub transfer.
This endpoint returns a list of inter-hub transfers, or if an ID is specified a single inter-hub transfer's details.

item.description: Inter-hub manifests are used to log and manage the transport of parcels between hubs.

request.description: A parcel can be updated by making a PATCH call to the parcels endpoint. The required field is the shipment ID (in the example this is 1071) and the list of parcels to be updated. The example includes all the fields that can possibly be updated.

item.name: Adding a POD to a shipment

request.description:
This endpoint captures a POD for a shipment and marks the shipment as "delivered" if it is not yet delivered.

There are various mutations of the body of the request:

1. Either `shipment_id` or `tracking_reference` should be supplied to identify the shipment the POD is captured for.
2. `date` is the timestamp of when the POD occurred. If left out, it will default to `now()`.
3. If you have a location of where the POD occurred, please provide a `lat` and `lng`.
4. `message` should define how the POD was captured. The options are:
  - "Recipient details entered"
  - "POD file(s) captured"
  - "Recipient details entered and POD file(s) captured"
5. `name`, `mobile_number` and `department` can be provided if the recipient details were entered.
6. The endpoint supports uploading images and/or pdfs (using `image_files` and `pdf_files` as in the example request). `file_size` should be the number of bytes and `data` should be the array of unsigned bytes to upload (Look up e.g. "Convert image to uint8 byte array" to see how it is done). For PDF's supply `display_name` as a human-readable name of the PDF file.

request.description:
The following fields are required:
- `collection_address`
- `collection_contact`
- `delivery_address`
- `delivery_contact`
- `parcels`
- either `service_level_code` or `service_level_id` (returned when retrieving rates)
- `is_return` (must be true)
- `short_tracking_reference` (the original shipment's short tracking reference)
- `custom_tracking_reference` (the original shipment's custom tracking reference, if applicable)

See the structure of addresses at the main section at the start of the documentation.

The `collection_contact` and `delivery_contact` requires at least an `email `and/or `mobile_number`.

`mute_notifications`: If this property is set to `true`, no SMS and/or email notifications will be sent on behalf of this shipment.

item.description:
This section describes the API calls that can be made by an administrator of a courier.

If you have administrator access, you can access any of the API endpoints in this section, as well as the Accounts API section. To do actions on behalf of an account, simply add `?account_id=X` as a query parameter on any of the API endpoints to e.g. make a shipment or retrieve the billing information for an account.

For the sandbox environment, the AWS access details below can be used to experiment with:

`"Authorization": "Bearer 37f112768d23494292f029e8855a6eac"`

To switch to using the production environment, you need to contact Shiplogic ([support@shiplogic.com](https://mailto:support@shiplogic.com)) for a production token.
